notes: This manifest is the canonical list of automated suites for Dungeoncrawler.
If the product also has language-native unit tests (e.g., PHPUnit), they can be included as suites here, but the PASS/FAIL contract must remain consistent.

suites.label: Role-based URL/ACL audit
suites.notes: Includes recursive URL validation by running scripts/site-validate-urls.py over the union of discovered crawl URLs and custom routes, then writing dungeoncrawler-validate.json (plus per-role validate.json files when role cookies are configured).

test_cases.description: site-audit role-url check returns 0 unexpected access violations
test_cases.command: cd /home/ubuntu/forseti.life/copilot-hq && ALLOW_PROD_QA=0 DUNGEONCRAWLER_BASE_URL=http://localhost bash scripts/site-audit-run.sh dungeoncrawler 2>&1 | tail -10

test_cases.description: Recursive URL validation writes validate.json from the crawl/route union and records at least one validated URL
test_cases.command:
cd /home/ubuntu/forseti.life/copilot-hq && rm -rf sessions/qa-dungeoncrawler/artifacts/auto-site-audit/latest && ALLOW_PROD_QA=0 DUNGEONCRAWLER_BASE_URL=http://localhost bash scripts/site-audit-run.sh dungeoncrawler >/tmp/dungeoncrawler-role-url-audit.log 2>&1 && python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
path = Path('sessions/qa-dungeoncrawler/artifacts/auto-site-audit/latest/dungeoncrawler-validate.json')
if not path.exists():
    raise SystemExit('missing validate.json')
payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
if int(payload.get('count', 0)) <= 0:
    raise SystemExit('validate.json contains no recursive URL results')
print(f"recursive validation count={payload['count']}")
PY

Creature more than 4 levels below party level (delta le-5) contributes 0 XP to encounter budget; treated as trivial; no validation error

Party size 1-3: Character Adjustment reduces XP budget per missing PC below 4; result ge 0 enforced

Mixed level-delta encounter: each creature XP cost computed independently then summed (delta-2 + delta0 + delta+1 = 20+40+60 = 120 XP)
Creature at delta gt+4: XP cost lookup returns null/not_defined with HTTP 200 (not 4xx error)

test_cases.description: Every hazard record has Stealth DC, AC, saving throw modifiers, Hardness, HP, and Broken Threshold defined (all non-null)
Hazard without minimum proficiency: all PCs auto-roll secret Perception vs Stealth DC when entering area; no active Search required
Hazard with minimum proficiency: only Searching PCs meeting minimum proficiency rank attempt detection; non-Searching or below-rank PCs excluded
Detect Magic reveals magical hazard (detected=true) regardless of minimum proficiency; disable_eligible=false unless normal disable conditions met
Passive-trigger hazard (trigger_type=passive): auto-activates when triggering condition met and detected=false
Detected passive-trigger hazard: does not auto-activate; party interaction required
Active-trigger hazard (trigger_type=active): activates only on explicit PC triggering action regardless of detection state
Simple hazard: hazard_type=simple, reactions=1, has_routine=false; resolves in single step
Complex hazard: hazard_type=complex, starts_encounter=true, has_routine=true, routine field non-null; performs routine each round
Complex hazard triggers while PCs already in encounter: hazard enters at own initiative using its Stealth modifier; inserted into existing initiative order
Disable a Device: action_cost=2, skill check vs hazard disable DC
Disable attempt blocked if detected=false and informed=false; returns error/prevention state
Disable attempt blocked for characters below min_disable_proficiency; no roll made
Complex hazard with multi-success: successes_required > 1; critical success increments success counter by 2
Hazard reset field is one of auto:<duration>, manual, or null (destroyed only); matches stat block spec
Hazard at or below Broken Threshold: state=broken, active=false, repairable=true
Hazard at 0 HP: state=destroyed, active=false, repairable=false
Attack reducing HP > 0: triggered=true; attack reducing HP to 0: triggered=false, state=destroyed
Standard hazard immune to non-object-targeting effects (immune=true); hazard with override flag accepts the effect
Magical hazard record has spell_level and counteract_dc both non-null; counteract follows ch09 rules
test_cases.notes: Activated 2026-04-12: dc-cr-spells-ch07 shipped (commit c720c1a34, release 20260412-dungeoncrawler-release-b).

test_cases.description: Hazard XP awarded only once per hazard per party; xp_awarded_flag set true after first overcome; subsequent overcomes return xp=0

Broken hazard (state=broken) cannot activate: ignores passive and active trigger conditions; no effect fires

test_cases.description: Spellcasting character shows spell slots keyed by level 1-10; counts match class progression table
Prepared caster: casting a spell decrements that slot level by 1; after rest all slots restored to max
Spontaneous caster: can cast known spell at any slot level >= spell base level; correct higher slot level decremented
Tradition field = one of {arcane, divine, occult, primal}; off-tradition spell cast attempt blocked
Prepared caster: daily preparation UI distinct from full known spell list; unprepared spell cast blocked
Spell attack = d20 + proficiency + key ability + item bonuses; Spell DC = 10 + proficiency + key ability; both use correct formula
Heightened spells: heighten_entries applied when cast at higher slot level; signature spells auto-heighten for spontaneous casters; prepared caster must explicitly prepare in higher slot
Cantrips: cast without spending slots (no slot change); effective_level updates to match character max spell level as it increases
Focus spells: cast costs 1 FP (not spell slot); Refocus 10-min action restores 1 FP up to max; spell_slot unchanged on focus cast

Data model: character entity has spell_slots{}, spellcasting_tradition, casting_type, spell_attack_modifier, spell_dc, focus_points, focus_points_max; spell type has traditions[], spell_level, heighten_entries[], is_cantrip, save_type, requires_attack_roll
Prepared caster cannot cast unprepared spell: attempt rejected with clear feedback
Proficiency rank change triggers recalculation: spell_attack_modifier and spell_dc update to reflect new rank
Four traditions present with distinct spell lists; spell selector filters by tradition; spells tagged with one or more traditions
Focus pool cap: fp_max cannot exceed 3 regardless of number of sources; adding fourth focus source leaves fp_max at 3

suites.label: Swashbuckler class: class record (HP10+Con, Dex key, Fortitude upgrades L3), panache binary state (speed+5, check bonus, style-skill trigger, Finisher-consumed), 5 styles (Battledancer/Braggart/Fencer/Gymnast/Wit with correct skill routing and feat grants), Precise Strike flat/dice precision scaling, Finisher requirements and partial-damage failure, Confident Finisher, Opportune Riposte reaction, Vivacious Speed scaling, Exemplary Finisher style effect, integration (panache UI, skill routing per style)
suites.notes: Security AC exemption confirmed: no new routes. All 16 TCs active. Regression risks: panache not cleared before Finisher outcome resolves; Finisher failure effect triggered on crit failure; Precise Strike flat bonus applied instead of dice on Finisher; Vivacious Speed half-value not rounded to nearest 5 ft; Exemplary Finisher firing on miss; wrong skill linked to style panache trigger.

test_cases.description: Class record: HP 10+Con/level, key_ability=Dex, Trained Fortitude (upgrades L3), Expert Reflex/Will, simple+martial weapons, light armor, class_dc tracked
Panache: binary state; panache=true grants speed_bonus=+5 status; lost immediately (before outcome) when Finisher performed; persists until encounter end otherwise
Panache active: +1 circumstance bonus to style-skill checks that would earn panache
Panache earned by success on style-specific skill check; failure produces no change
GM-award panache mechanism exists for daring non-standard actions vs Very Hard DC; no auto-grant pathway
Styles: each grants Trained in correct skill; correct panache trigger linked; Battledancer grants Fascinating Performance; Wit grants Bon Mot feat
Precise Strike non-Finisher: with panache + agile/finesse weapon, flat precision bonus = +2/+3/+4/+5/+6 at L1/5/9/13/17
Precise Strike Finisher: precision dice = 2d6/3d6/4d6/5d6/6d6 at L1/5/9/13/17; panache consumed before outcome
Finisher: blocked if panache=false; after Finisher, attack-trait actions blocked for rest of turn
Finisher failure effect: failure -> partial damage (flat); crit failure -> NO failure effect triggered (distinct from failure)
Confident Finisher L1: 1-action; success=full precision dice; failure=flat_half_value (no dice rolled); action_cost=1
Opportune Riposte L3 reaction: triggers on foe crit failure on Strike vs swashbuckler; player chooses melee Strike or Disarm
Vivacious Speed L3: with panache +10/+15/+20/+25/+30 ft at L3/7/11/15/19; without panache half rounded to nearest 5 ft
Exemplary Finisher L9: fires only on Finisher hit; applies style-specific effect matching selected style
Integration: panache state indicator visible in encounter UI; auto-removed when Finisher action taken (before resolution)
Integration: each style uses only its correct skill for panache generation (Performance/Intimidation/Deception/Athletics/Diplomacy for Battledancer/Braggart/Fencer/Gymnast/Wit)

suites.label: APG equipment: weapons (Sword Cane concealment, Bola trip, Daikyu mount restriction), adventuring gear (Detective Kit, Dueling Cape, Net dual-mode), alchemical items (Blight Bomb, Dread Ampoule, Crystal Shards, Focus Cathartic counteract scaling, Sinew-Shock Serum, Olfactory Obfuscator, Leadenleg, Cerulean Scourge, Timeless Salts, Universal Solvent, Forensic Dye), consumable magic (Candle of Revealing, Dust of Corpse Animation 4-cap, Potion of Retaliation, Terrifying Ammunition, Oil of Unlife), permanent magic (Glamorous Buckler, Victory Plate, Slates pair destruction, Four-Ways Dogslicer, Winged Rune, Rod of Cancellation 2d6-cooldown), APG snares, integration edge cases
suites.notes: Security AC exemption confirmed: no new routes. All 16 TCs active. Key regression risks: Bola Trip not offered post-hit; Daikyu non-left mount firing not blocked; Net immobilize on non-crit; Focus Cathartic counteract modifier not tier-scaled; Dust undead 4-cap not enforced; Terrifying Ammunition Frightened floor not held at 1; Rod of Cancellation cooldown not rolling 2d6 hours; Slate destruction not propagating to partner.

test_cases.description: New weapons: Sword Cane, Bola, Daikyu in weapon selector with correct traits and proficiency grouping
Sword Cane: weapon identity not revealed in social inspection by default; investigation check required to expose
Bola: successful hit offers Trip sub-action via standard Athletics vs CMD
Daikyu: while mounted non-left-side firing blocked or flagged; left-side or dismounted firing allowed
Adventuring gear: Detective Kit +1 item investigation checks; Dueling Cape Interact-to-deploy +1 AC/Feint; Net dual-mode (Grapple 10ft / crit-immobilize, Escape DC 16, ally Interact to remove)
Alchemical bombs: Blight Bomb (poison+persistent+splash); Dread Ampoule (Enfeebled 2 until next turn, fear/emotion traits); Crystal Shards (caltrops or handholds context-dependent)
Counteract consumables: Focus Cathartic (Confused/Stupefied, 1 condition per use); Sinew-Shock Serum (Clumsy/Enfeebled); counteract modifier +6/+8/+19/+28 at L2/4/12/18
Alchemical utility: Olfactory Obfuscator (concealment vs scent detectors); Leadenleg (Speed reduction, Fortitude resists); Forensic Dye (tracking mark, Seek/Track bonus)
Preservation/poison: Timeless Salts (7-day decay prevention flag); Universal Solvent (auto-counteract sovereign glue); Cerulean Scourge (3-stage poison stages stored per item entry)
Consumable magic: Candle of Revealing (invisible -> concealed not observed); Dust of Corpse Animation (1 undead 1-min duration, max 4 total cap enforced)
Consumable magic: Potion of Retaliation (damage type set at craft time); Terrifying Ammunition (Frightened floor=1 until concentrate action); Oil of Unlife (heals undead, no harm to living)
Permanent magic: Glamorous Buckler (Feint bonus only while raised, dazzle daily limit); Victory Plate (kill log persists, resistance grant); Rope of Climbing (command animation)

Permanent magic: Slates of Distant Letters (pair-crafted, destruction propagates to partner, 25-word limit, 1-hour cooldown per slate); Four-Ways Dogslicer (3 rune slots Interact-to-swap, 1d6 activation damage)

Permanent magic: Winged Rune (5-min fly 1/hour); Wand of Overflowing Life (bonus heal); Wand of Snowfields (difficult terrain); Urn of Ashes (doomed reaction once/rest); Rod of Cancellation (permanent cancel, cooldown rolls 2d6 hours)
APG snares in snare selector alongside CRB snares: Engulfing Snare (Hardness 5 HP 30, Escape DC 31); Flare Snare (signal only, damage=0)
Infiltrators Accessory: social-context concealment only; does not suppress magical detection

suites.label: APG general and skill feats: non-skill general feats (Hireling Manager, Improvised Repair, Keen Follower, Pick Up the Pace, Prescient Planner/Consumable, Skitter, Thorough Search, True Perception, Home in Every Port, Caravan Leader, Incredible Scout), skill feats by category (Acrobatics, Athletics, Crafting, Deception, Diplomacy, Intimidation, Lore, Medicine), multi-skill feats (Group Aid, Fascinating Spellcaster, Oddity Identification, Recognize Spell, Scare to Death, Shameless Request, Trick Magic Item), Nature feats, system-level general feat availability
suites.notes: Security AC exemption confirmed: no new routes. All 20 TCs active. Regression risks: Prescient Planner item eligibility filter too broad/narrow; Aerobatics Mastery missing rank gate; Bon Mot self-penalty on crit fail not applied; No Cause for Alarm missing 1-hour immunity; Ward Medic patient cap not enforced; Group Aid stacking with other circumstance bonuses; Scare to Death frequency or Frightened-4 gate not enforced; Trick Magic Item routing wrong tradition-to-skill.

test_cases.description: Non-skill general feats: Hireling Manager (+2 circ to hireling checks), Improvised Repair (shoddy state), Keen Follower (scales 3/4 by expertise), Pick Up the Pace (+20 min group Hustle) — prereqs and bonuses enforced
Prescient Planner: retroactive procurement (level ≤ half char level, Bulk constrained, not weapon/armor/alchemical/magic); Prescient Consumable extends to consumables (prereq enforced)
Skitter: Crawl up to half Speed; Thorough Search: double time → +2 circ to Seek; True Perception: permanent true seeing using Perception modifier for counteract
A Home in Every Port: free lodging for self+6 per settlement; Caravan Leader (prereq Pick Up the Pace): group Hustle → longest member duration +20 min; Incredible Scout: +2 ally initiative
Acrobatics skill feats: Acrobatic Performer (Acrobatics substitutes Perform); Aerobatics Mastery (+2 to Maneuver in Flight, Master=2→1 action DC+5, Legendary=3→1 DC+10) — rank gates enforced

Athletics skill feats: Lead Climber (crit-fail climb → ally regular fail only; double crit = both fall); Water Sprint (cross water during Stride, must end on solid; straight path at Master, any path at Legendary)

Crafting skill feats: Crafter's Appraisal (Crafting for Identify Magic on items); Improvise Tool (Repair without kit; blocked if no raw materials); Rapid Affixture (1 min at Master, 3 actions at Legendary)
Deception: Doublespeak — hidden message in conversation; long-term allies auto-understand; others: Perception vs. Deception DC; only crit success decodes (not detects)
Diplomacy: Bon Mot — 1-action; status penalty to Perception + Will saves; target removes via verbal/concentrate retort; crit fail → penalty on caster instead
Diplomacy: No Cause for Alarm — 3-action; reduces Frightened in 10-ft emanation on success/crit; 1-hour immunity after use

Intimidation: Terrifying Resistance (+1 circ to saves vs. spells from Demoralized creature, 24-hr window); Lore: Battle Assessment (Recall Knowledge reveals highest damage modifier or attack bonus on success)
Medicine: Continual Recovery (Battle Medicine immunity → 10 min); Robust Recovery (crit = max HP next check + temp HP); Ward Medic (≤4 simultaneous patients); Godless Healing (no deity req)
Group Aid: Aid up to 4 creatures simultaneously; preparation action taken prior turn; circumstance bonus applied (no stacking with other circumstance)
Fascinating Spellcaster: Fascinate using spell level to set DC; targets make Perception vs. DC; spellcasting prereq enforced
Oddity Identification: +2 circ to Recall Knowledge for curses/haunts/oddities only; Recognize Spell: non-primary tradition at -2; ritual prep identifiable
Scare to Death (Uncommon): 1/day frequency enforced; Frightened ≥4 prereq at activation; Fortitude save; fail → massive damage + unconscious chain; Uncommon gate blocks non-authorized
Shameless Request: attitude requirement lowers by 1 step (even hostile allowed); Trick Magic Item: tradition-to-skill routing (Arcana/Nature/Occultism/Religion) matches item tradition
Nature: Bonded Animal (deep bond with one specific non-combat animal; complex commands); Train Animal (prereq Bonded Animal; 1 week per trick)
System: APG general feats appear in general feat selector for any character class/ancestry; only level and stat prerequisites enforced (no class/ancestry gate)

Feat prerequisite chain enforcement: Caravan Leader requires Pick Up the Pace; Prescient Consumable requires Prescient Planner; Aerobatics Mastery rank gates — all block out-of-order selection in character creation

suites.label: Animal companion system: content type (all fields stored), initialization at young for eligible classes, advancement tiers (young→mature→nimble/savage), Command an Animal 1-action 2-actions-on-success, no-command Stride/Strike repeat, companion vs familiar distinction (full combat stats), 0 HP unconscious not dead, species base stats, flier aerial movement, Command range/line-of-effect gate
suites.notes: Security AC exemption: companion data is character-scoped, no PII, command resolution server-validated. All 13 TCs active (10 original + 3 added for specializations, mount rules, species completeness). Regression risks: non-eligible class gaining companion; advancement not recalculating Mature stats; Command success giving wrong action count; 0 HP setting dead flag instead of unconscious; familiar record incorrectly getting attack entries; species selection leaving fields null; duplicate specializations applied; non-Large species flagged as mount-eligible.

test_cases.description: Companion content type: all required fields present (companion_id, character_id, companion_type, size, speeds, senses, HP, AC, saves, attack entries, advancement_level); no null required fields
Companion initialized at 'young' advancement for Ranger/Druid/Beastmaster Archetype; non-eligible classes blocked from companion creation
Advancement: young → mature → nimble or savage; Mature tier recalculates AC, saves, HP, attack modifier, and damage per Mature Animal Companion table
Command an Animal: costs 1 action; success vs. DC 15 (or creature Will DC) → companion takes 2 actions that turn
No Command issued: companion limited to Stride and/or Strike matching prior turn behavior only
Companion vs. familiar distinction: companion record has attack_bonus, damage_entries, AC, saves; familiar record lacks attack entries
Companion at 0 HP: state = unconscious (not destroyed/dead); permanent death requires explicit GM flag or failed recovery
Species selection sets base stats, size, speed, senses, and natural attack type; fields not manually editable unless advanced
Flier companion (eagle, bat): fly_speed present; elevation tracked in combat; plunging strike available at height
Command an Animal blocked if companion outside communicable range or line-of-effect broken; edge case documented

Specializations: companion may have at most one specialization (bully/defender/racer/scout/tracker); each grants correct stat delta (attack_mod_bonus, ac_bonus, speed_bonus, skill_bonuses) and granted_action

Mount rules: only Large/Huge-eligible species (horse, deer, ape@Mature) can be ridden; Stride (Mounted) costs 1 action; Command Mount DC is 5 lower; rider falls on mount 0 HP or prone with correct fall damage calculation

Species completeness: all 11 CRB species (bear, bird, cat, wolf, horse, snake, ape, crocodile, deer, dog, frog) available for selection; each has required fields: size, speed, senses, hp_per_level, base_ac, base_saves, attacks[]

suites.label: Oracle class mechanics: class record (HP/Cha/Will Expert), divine spontaneous spellcasting (material→somatic), cantrip auto-heighten, sorcerer spell slot progression, Signature Spells L3, mystery selection (8 options locked at creation), revelation spells (cursebound trait, 2 FP focus pool), 4-stage curse machine (basic→minor→moderate→overwhelmed), curse overwhelmed blocking, refocus moderate→minor reset, daily reset to basic, curse irremovable, per-mystery curse progressions and granted spells, focus pool cap 2, edge cases (0 FP block, initial cast path)
suites.notes: Security AC exemption confirmed: no new routes. All 17 TCs active. Regression risks: material component flag not cleared for oracle spells; cantrip heighten formula using floor instead of ceil; mystery not locked after creation; curse stage advancing incorrectly (e.g. skipping moderate); overwhelmed not blocking revelation casting; refocus resetting to basic instead of minor; remove_curse succeeding on oracle; focus pool auto-growing beyond 2 without feat; TC-ORC-06 first revelation spell not forced by mystery.

test_cases.description: Class record: HP 8+Con/level, key ability Cha, Expert Will, Trained Fort/Ref; divine spontaneous spellcasting; material_component_required=false; 5 cantrips + 2 first-level spells
Cantrips auto-heighten to ceil(level/2); spell slots per day match sorcerer progression (TABLE 2-3)
Signature Spells at L3: one signature spell slot per accessible spell level; selected spell castable at any higher slot
Mystery selection: 8 options shown at creation; after selection, mystery locked (change blocked)
Revelation spells: cursebound trait on all; focus pool starts at 2 FP; Refocus 10 min restores 1 FP
Revelation spells at L1: initial (forced, mystery-specific) + one domain spell (player choice from domain list)
Curse stage machine: stage 0 (basic passive) → minor → moderate → overwhelmed; each revelation cast advances by 1; overwhelmed blocks all revelation casting
Overwhelmed state: revelation_cast and sustain_revelation both blocked; resets at daily prep
Refocus at moderate or higher: curse resets to minor (not basic) + FP += 1
Daily prep (8hr rest): curse resets to basic stage
Curse irremovable: remove_curse targeting oracle returns failure; curse_stage unchanged
Curse traits: [curse, divine, necromancy] all present
Per-mystery curse progressions: all 8 mysteries have distinct 4-stage curse effects (not shared generic widget); UI renders them uniquely
Per-mystery granted content: all 8 mysteries have initial (locked) + advanced + greater revelation spells + domain spell choices; advanced/greater accessible at correct levels
Focus pool cap: oracle.focus_pool_max = 2 by default; grows only when focus-spell feats taken; hard cap 3 per standard rules
Edge: revelation cast at 0 FP → blocked regardless of curse stage
Edge: initial revelation cast activates curse at basic stage; first cast of day succeeds and sets initial curse state

suites.label: Witch class mechanics: class record (HP 6+Con/Int key/Expert Will/unarmored), patron theme (7 themes locked at creation, grants tradition/skill/hex cantrip/familiar spell), mandatory familiar (auto-created, holds all spells, bonus abilities L1/6/12/18, death preserves spells), prepared spellcasting via familiar commune, spell repertoire (10 cantrips+5 1st+patron spell, +2/level), familiar scroll absorption and familiar-to-familiar sharing, hexes (1 FP, one per turn, focus pool 1), hex cantrips (0 FP, auto-heighten, no slot usage), Refocus via commune, Witch Lessons tiered feats (Basic/Greater/Major), notable hexes (Cackle extend, Evil Eye status, Phase Familiar), Cackle edge case
suites.notes: Security AC exemption confirmed: no new routes. All 18 TCs active. Regression risks: patron theme not locked after creation; familiar not auto-created; familiar death erasing spells; hex cantrip consuming prepared cantrip slot; second hex per turn not blocked; Cackle incorrectly counted as second hex cast; Lessons not gated by tier level; Evil Eye not removed on Will success.

test_cases.description: Class record: HP 6+Con/level, key ability Int, Trained Fort/Ref, Expert Will, unarmored proficiency only
Patron theme: 7 options shown at L1; after selection locked; grants tradition, patron_skill (trained), hex cantrip, familiar first spell
Familiar: mandatory, auto-created at character creation, cannot be dismissed, all spells stored in familiar
Familiar: bonus ability unlocked at L1, L6, L12, L18; correct count at each milestone
Familiar death: spells list preserved; new familiar with same spells restored at next daily prep
Witch prepared spellcasting: spellcasting_type=prepared; preparation UI shows commune step; no spontaneous casting
Spell repertoire: 10 cantrips + 5 first-level + 1 patron spell at start; +2 player-chosen spells per level-up
Familiar learns from scrolls (1 hr each, consumes scroll); familiar-to-familiar via Learn a Spell (both present); direct prep from foreign familiar blocked
Hexes: cost 1 FP; hex_cantrip cost 0 FP; only one hex per turn any type; initial focus_pool = 1
Hex cantrips: auto-heighten to ceil(level/2); using hex_cantrip blocks second hex that turn
Hex cantrips do not occupy prepared cantrip slots; separate pool from prepared cantrips
Refocus: 10 min commune with familiar; restores 1 FP
Witch Lessons: tiered feat mechanism (Basic L2/Greater L6/Major L10); each lesson grants 1 hex + 1 familiar spell
Basic Lessons catalog: 5 lessons (Dreams/Elements/Life/Protection/Vengeance); each grants correct hex + spell pair; accessible at L2+
Greater Lessons (Mischief/Shadow/Snow) gated at L6+; Major Lessons (Death/Renewal) gated at L10+; correct hex/spell pairs
Notable hexes: Cackle extends active hex +1 round (not a second cast); Evil Eye 0 FP -2 status removed on Will success; Phase Familiar incorporeal limited to 1 damage negation
Integration: familiar.spells grows by exactly 2 player-chosen spells per level-up event
Edge: Cackle extending a hex cantrip is valid; Cackle does not count as casting a second hex; one_hex_per_turn rule not violated

suites.label: APG Focus Spells — oracle revelation cursebound, witch hexes (Evil Eye/Cackle/Phase Familiar), bard compositions, ranger warden spells, focus pool cap 3

test_cases.description: Oracle: each mystery (8 total) defines revelation/advanced/greater/domain spells
Oracle: cursebound trait on all revelation spells; cast → curse_stage += 1
Oracle: 4-stage curse progression per mystery with unique effects (not shared generic condition)
Oracle focus pool starts at 2 FP (not 1); cap 3 via standard expansion
Witch hex Evil Eye: 0 FP cost, -2 status penalty, sustained, ends on Will save success
Witch hex Cackle: extends active hex +1 round; fails gracefully with no active hex; does not start new hex
Witch hex Phase Familiar: reaction on incoming damage; one hit negated; incorporeality temporary (single use)
Witch focus pool starts at 1 FP; hex cantrip counts as hex-used-this-turn; second hex same turn blocked
Evil Eye + Cackle edge: Cackle after Evil Eye hex cantrip is valid extension (not second hex cast)
Bard compositions: Hymn of Healing (2 HP/round sustained), Song of Strength (+2 circ Athletics), Gravity Weapon (damage dice count; doubles vs Large+)
Ranger warden spells: primal focus pool, Refocus = 10 min in nature, effects per spell entry
Focus pool expansion cap at 3 across all sources (oracle/witch/bard/ranger)

suites.label: APG Rituals — stat block completeness, multiple secondary casters, rare/uncommon gate, extensibility, edge cases (0-secondary, name collision)

test_cases.description: Ritual system accepts APG entries without structural changes; APG + CRB in same table/UI
Ritual stat block completeness: level, casting time, cost, primary check skill+proficiency, secondary checks, targets, effect all stored
Multiple secondary casters pattern: secondary_checks stored as array; UI shows each role+skill
Rare/Uncommon rituals require GM approval; non-approved character cannot initiate; GM override unlocks
Primary check modifier stored per-ritual (data-driven, not hardcoded in UI)
Edge: ritual with 0 secondary casters renders correctly; no secondary-caster error
Edge: APG ritual same name as CRB ritual differentiated by book_id composite key

suites.label: APG New Spells — 8 named spells (Animate Dead, Blood Vendetta, Déjà Vu, Final Sacrifice, Heat Metal, Mad Monkeys, Pummeling Rubble, Vomit Swarm), system extensibility, heighten scaling via data-driven parameter tables

test_cases.description: APG spells added to existing traditions (arcane/divine/occult/primal) without structural changes; heightened variants use parameter tables
Animate Dead: 3-action (M+S+V); creates one undead ≤ level cap per rank table; sustained max 1 min; auto-ends if not sustained; no damage/save
Animate Dead: summoned creature obeys summoned-trait constraints; disappears when spell ends; no persistent undead after duration

Blood Vendetta: reaction trigger on piercing/slashing/bleed; base 2d6 persistent bleed on attacker; Will save outcomes (crit success=none, success=half, failure=full+weakness 1, crit failure=double+weakness 1)
Blood Vendetta: undead/construct casters blocked from casting; heighten +2d6 per +2 ranks; scaling parameter-driven
Déjà Vu: Will save; on fail engine records target's next-turn action sequence; following turn target must repeat sequence; illegal action → substitute + Stupefied 1 that turn; no damage
Final Sacrifice: permanent minion required; minion slain immediately; 6d6 fire basic Reflex 20 ft; cold/water trait minion → cold damage; temporary minion auto-fails
Final Sacrifice: mindless sacrifice = no evil trait; non-mindless = evil trait recorded; heighten +2d6 per +1 rank; parameter-driven
Heat Metal: unattended target → no save; worn/carried/metal creature → 4d6 fire + 2d4 persistent fire Reflex; held item → Release action improves degree by 1 step

Heat Metal: persistent fire state tracked on item; save outcome table (crit success=none, success=half+none persistent, failure=full+full, crit failure=double); heighten +2d6 initial +1d4 persistent per rank
Mad Monkeys: sustained up to 1 min; mode (Burglary/Din/Gymnastics) locked at cast; area repositionable 5 ft per Sustain action
Mad Monkeys modes: Burglary Steal (Thievery=spell_dc-10); Din deafened durations per Fortitude save tier; Gymnastics flat check 5 vs. manipulate on fail; stolen items drop at spell end
Mad Monkeys: Calm Emotions active in same area suppresses mischief effects while both overlap
Pummeling Rubble: 2d4 bludgeoning 15-ft cone; Reflex; failure=full+pushed 5 ft; crit failure=double+pushed 10 ft; movement directly away from caster; blocked by walls
Pummeling Rubble: heighten +2d4 per +1 spell rank; parameter table driven
Vomit Swarm: 2d8 piercing 30-ft cone; basic Reflex; Sickened 1 only on fail/crit fail; no persistent swarm entity; heighten +1d8 per rank
System: all scaling spells use stored heighten parameter tables (not hardcoded rank checks); adding new heighten rank requires only data entry

suites.label: Ancestry Traits — auto-assign on character creation, hasTraits logic, persistence, ACL (read-only by players), trait dedup, case-sensitivity, rejection of unknown/direct-mutation attempts

test_cases.description: Ancestry catalog defines creature traits per ancestry; Dwarf → [Dwarf, Humanoid], Human → [Human, Humanoid], Elf → [Elf, Humanoid]
Dwarf character creation auto-assigns traits [Dwarf, Humanoid] without additional player action; confirmed via GET character state
Traits persist across session reload; traits array not cleared after navigate-away and re-fetch
hasTraits(dwarf_id, [Humanoid]) returns true when character has that trait
hasTraits(dwarf_id, [Elf]) returns false when character does not have that trait
Character state API exposes traits field; player 200, admin 200, anon 403
Mixed-heritage (Half-Elf) receives union of Human+Elf traits with no duplicates — skip and flag to PM if Half-Elf not in catalog
Duplicate trait assignment is idempotent; assigning Humanoid twice yields exactly one Humanoid entry
Unknown/non-catalog trait string rejected with HTTP 4xx and error message containing Unknown trait: X
Trait comparison is case-sensitive; hasTraits([humanoid]) returns false when canonical is Humanoid (PM decision commit 576262c5)
GET traits for non-existent character returns HTTP 404 structured error, not 500
Invalid (non-catalog) trait rejected at storage with non-200 or explicit error; trait not silently persisted
Trait data readable by session participants: player 200, admin 200, anon 403
Direct client-side PATCH/POST to traits array rejected with 403 or 405; traits unchanged after attempt

suites.label: Character Leveling — level-up trigger (session milestone), class features auto-apply, ability boosts, skill increases, feat selection with prerequisites, persistence, ACL (player own/GM any), edge cases (max level, no-milestone, skip-level, concurrent)

test_cases.description: Session milestone trigger advances character level N → N+1; GET state confirms level incremented
test_cases.notes: Requires Dev-provided test helper to set milestone state

test_cases.description: Level-up response includes pending_choices/advancement section with class features, feat slots, ability boosts for new level
Auto-apply class features with no player choice (Fighter level 3 Shield Block or equivalent); feature present in GET state with no pending prompt
Level 5 ability boost: 4 boost slots presented, player submits 4 distinct abilities, all scores increment +2 in GET state
Skill increase at applicable level: one skill proficiency rank raised by one step, other skills unchanged
Feat slot at level 2: catalog filtered by prerequisites; selected feat persists in GET state character.feats
Level-up idempotent: second level-up attempt before new milestone returns 4xx; character level unchanged
Full level-up (all choices resolved) persists across session reload: level, features, boosts, skill ranks, feats all intact
Level-up attempt at level 20 returns 4xx with 'Already at maximum level' message
Level-up attempt without session milestone reached returns 4xx with clear rejection message
Skip-level attempt (level 1 → 3 directly) rejected; each level must be applied in sequence
No-choice level: class feature with no player selection (e.g., +10 HP) auto-applied without prompting
Ability boost past 18 at post-creation level milestone: boost applied (PF2e: post-creation may exceed 18); no false rejection
Non-existent character ID returns HTTP 404 structured JSON error, not PHP exception or HTML page
Concurrent level-up POSTs for same character: character ends at exactly N+1 (not N+2); no partial/corrupt state
test_cases.notes: May require PHPUnit-level concurrency test; flag to Dev if playwright concurrent helper unavailable

test_cases.description: Missing class advancement data returns structured error, not unhandled 500 or PHP exception
test_cases.notes: Requires Dev-provided stub class fixture with incomplete advancement table

test_cases.description: ACL: player 200 own character, player 403 other character, admin 200 any, anon 403 for level-up endpoint
Anonymous users: GET and POST level-up endpoint both return 403
Admin force-apply level without milestone; admin reset level-up state back to pre-level; both operations succeed
test_cases.notes: Requires PM/Dev confirmation of force-apply and reset endpoint routes

suites.label: Alchemist Class — identity/INT key ability, research fields (Bomber/Chirurgeon/Mutagenist), infused reagents, Advanced Alchemy, Quick Alchemy, formula book, level-gated features (5/7/9/11/13/15/17), additive feats, cross-player ACL, expiry enforcement
suites.command: ./vendor/bin/phpunit --filter AlchemistClassTest --filter AlchemistResearchFieldTest --filter AlchemistInfusedReagentsTest --filter AlchemistAdvancedAlchemyTest --filter AlchemistQuickAlchemyTest --filter AlchemistFormulaBookTest --filter AlchemistLevelFeaturesTest --filter AlchemistFeatProgressionTest --filter AlchemistAdditiveFeatTest --log-junit test-results/dc-cr-class-alchemist/junit.xml
suites.notes: Uses PHPUnit (module-test-suite), not Playwright. Security AC exemption — no new routes. Dependency: dc-cr-equipment-system must be stable for Advanced/Quick Alchemy item creation tests (TC-ALC-08 through TC-ALC-21).

test_cases.description: Alchemist exists as selectable class in character creation
Class description references alchemical items, not spellcasting
INT enforced as key ability; class DC references INT
Starting HP = 8 + CON mod at level 1; level 2 doubles
Research field: Bomber/Chirurgeon/Mutagenist accepted and stored at level 1
Research field locked after level 1 — change attempt rejected
Invalid research field rejected with validation error
Bomber field: Advanced Alchemy produces bombs
Chirurgeon field: Advanced Alchemy produces healing elixirs; Medicine bonus granted
Mutagenist field: Advanced Alchemy produces mutagens
Infused reagent count = level + INT mod (minimum 1)
Infused reagents refresh at daily preparations
Reagents consumed by Advanced and Quick Alchemy actions
Quick Alchemy blocked with 0 reagents; clear error referencing reagent count
Advanced Alchemy creates items during daily prep from formula book; no gold cost
Advanced Alchemy item level cap enforced (item level <= character level)
Advanced Alchemy items expire at next daily prep; afflictions persist
Quick Alchemy: creates 1 item, consumes 1 reagent
Quick Alchemy item expires at start of alchemist's next turn

suites.label: Sorcerer Class — identity/stats (HP6+CON, Charisma key, Expert Will, unarmored), Bloodline selection (11 options, tradition-locked, immutable post-creation), spontaneous Spell Repertoire (charisma-based, cantrips+slots), Signature Spells at L3, Focus Pool (start 1 cap 3, blood magic trigger), CLASS_ADVANCEMENT L1–L19 milestones, CLASS_FEATS progression, auth guards
suites.notes: Sorcerer is the only spontaneous bloodline caster. Key regression risks: Expert Will at L1 (unique vs Trained default); Bloodline immutable post-creation; spell_repertoire type=spontaneous vs prepared; Signature Spells one-per-rank gained at L3; blood_magic trigger on bloodline spells and cantrips; FOCUS_POOLS expand_per_source with cap=3. 11 bloodlines implemented (feature.md stated 8 — dev extended with elemental/genie/nymph). Cantrips stored as CLASS_FEATS level=0 entries (15 cantrips); not a bug — always-available cantrips per PF2e design.

test_cases.description: Sorcerer appears in class selection list during character creation; is a valid selectable class

Armor proficiency: unarmored only at level 1; no light/medium/heavy

Each bloodline maps to correct tradition: draconic/imperial→arcane, angelic/undead/demonic→divine, fey/elemental/nymph→primal, aberrant/hag→occult, genie→arcane
Spell repertoire type = spontaneous (not prepared); known spells castable multiple times per slot

CLASS_ADVANCEMENT L1: Bloodline + Spell Repertoire auto-features granted at character creation
CLASS_ADVANCEMENT L19: Legendary Spellcaster + Bloodline Perfection (10th-level slot) granted

suites.label: Wizard Class — identity/stats (HP6+CON, Intelligence key, Expert Will, unarmored), Arcane School (8 specialist+Universalist, extra slot per rank, focus spells), Arcane Thesis (5 options: Spell Blending/Substitution/Improved Familiar/Experimental Spellshaping/Staff Nexus), Arcane Bond (bonded item or familiar), Drain Bonded Item (once/day free action), Spellbook (10 spells + 5 cantrips, prepared arcane, 2 spells/level), ARCANE_SCHOOLS constant (8 schools), FOCUS_POOLS (start=1 cap=3), CLASS_ADVANCEMENT L1–L19, CLASS_FEATS L1–L20 (33 feats), auth guards

Cantrip from chosen wellspring tradition stored as at-will innate spell with correct tradition tag
Cantrip spell level equals ceil(character_level / 2); verified at levels 1 and 7
Wellspring cantrip has no per-day use cap; casting does not decrement a use counter
First World Magic innate spell tradition overridden to wellspring_tradition when Wellspring heritage active
All gnome ancestry feat innate spells with tradition=primal are overridden to wellspring_tradition
Tradition override is scoped to gnome ancestry feat innate spells only; class spells retain original tradition

dc-cr-gnome-heritage-fey-touched.label: Fey-touched Gnome gains fey trait in addition to Gnome and Humanoid
Player selects primal cantrip at heritage selection; stored as at-will innate primal spell
Cantrip spell level equals ceil(character_level / 2); verified at levels 1, 5, and 9
Innate cantrip has no use-per-day cap; casting does not decrement a use counter
Daily cantrip swap is a 10-minute concentrate activity; replacement must be from primal list
One swap allowed per day; swap_used_today resets to false on daily preparation
Second same-day swap attempt returns error: 'Daily cantrip swap already used'
fey trait is additive; character.traits contains Gnome, Humanoid, and fey (all three)

dc-cr-first-world-magic.label: First World Magic appears in level 1 gnome ancestry feat list
Player selects primal cantrip at feat acquisition; stored as at-will innate spell with correct attributes
First World Magic cantrip is fixed after acquisition; no swap action available in UI
Innate cantrip has no per-day use cap; casting does not decrement a use counter
Cantrip spell level equals ceil(character_level / 2); verified at levels 1, 5, and 9
Wellspring Gnome heritage overrides cantrip tradition to wellspring_tradition at feat acquisition
First World Magic + Fey-touched Heritage each grant separate at-will innate cantrip entries; same spell allowed as duplicate

dc-cr-burrow-elocutionist.label: Burrow Elocutionist appears in Gnome ancestry feat list and is selectable at level 1
Character with feat can converse with burrowing creatures; dialogue available and understandable
Non-burrowing creatures do not gain special dialogue channel from feat; effect scoped to burrowing tag only
Character without Burrow Elocutionist feat cannot use burrow-language interaction

dc-cr-animal-accomplice.label: Animal Accomplice appears in Gnome feat list and is selectable at feat 1
Selecting Animal Accomplice feat creates a familiar record using standard familiar rules
Familiar type chosen from valid catalog; invalid type rejected by selection flow
Non-spellcasting gnome character receives familiar from Animal Accomplice feat
Gnome without Animal Accomplice feat does not gain familiar via this feat path
Server rejects familiar assignment with invalid/unknown familiar type
